message:
fix(cursor): stop DataDebris particles from corrupting shared mouse MotionValues
and remove isMounted antipattern that triggered react-hooks/set-state-in-effect

Two fixes in one:

1. DataDebris MotionValue corruption: The DataDebris component passed the
   shared mouseX/mouseY MotionValues to each particle via `style={{ x, y }}`
   while simultaneously animating those same properties with
   `animate={{ x: [...], y: [...] }}`. Framer-motion writes keyframe values
   directly back into MotionValues supplied via style, so 5 particles were
   continuously overwriting mouseX/mouseY with near-zero drift offsets. This
   caused the custom cursor to fly off-screen over any <pre>/<code> element.

   Fix: wrap particles in a single motion.div container positioned by the
   shared MotionValues, with individual particles animating independently.

2. React 19 lint violation: `useEffect(() => { setIsMounted(true) }, [])`
   triggers react-hooks/set-state-in-effect. The isMounted guard was
   unnecessary — the other three FrankenSuite sites never had it, and the
   cursor already gates rendering on isVisible (set by mouse events, which
   only fire client-side). Removed the isMounted state entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
